text:
BOTTOM LINE — Violent de-risking, AI complex liquidated, defensives bid. The blowout May payrolls print and 10-year yield spike to 4.54% forced a “good news is bad news” reset in Fed-cut odds, triggering mechanical de-rating in long-duration growth and AI-chip exposure. Semiconductor leadership cracked hard as the SOX and SOXX collapsed while staples, utilities, healthcare, and financials absorbed rotation, leaving a classic factor shock: growth and momentum sold, value and defensive quality accumulated.

INDICES — VIX jumped to 21.5, firmly above its 20-day at 17.1, confirming a volatility regime shift higher. DIA -1.35% > RSP -1.42% > IJR -1.84% > IJH -2.00% > SPY -2.58% > IWM -3.55% > QQQ -4.80% > SOXX -10.44%, with STYLE showing SMALL > LARGE by 0.74pp on a relative basis despite absolute small-cap pain. The standout cross-asset move was SLV -8.08% alongside GLD -3.65% as precious metals were liquidated with higher real yields and a stronger UUP +0.65%.

VOLUME — SOXX 2.5x, QQQ 2.3x, and GLD 2.0x highlighted where de-risking was concentrated, while IJH 1.1x, RSP 1.1x, and IJR 0.9x showed relatively muted attention in the broader equity beta. SPY volume near the 1.6x composite and QQQ 2.3x with sharp price declines point to distribution rather than a low-conviction dip-buy, especially with XLK 2.1x and XLC 1.8x confirming forced tech and comms unwinds.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLP Staples +1.71%, XLU Utilities +0.93%, XLRE Real Estate +0.68%, XLV Healthcare +0.61%, XLF Financials +0.21%, XLI Industrials -1.12%, XLC Comms -1.27%, XLE Energy -1.84%, XLB Materials -1.92%, XLY Discretionary -2.05%, XLK Technology -6.66%. MSOS +7.25% and KIE +2.97% led industries while SOXX -10.44% and WGMI -11.20% anchored the losers, encapsulating a sharp barbell between defensives/niche trades and high-beta AI/crypto-adjacent risk. Money-in is clearly rotating toward staples, utilities, healthcare, and insurers, while money-out is concentrated in semis, AI hardware, silver miners, and speculative growth. This was a defensive risk-off rotation, not a healthy “buy the dip,” with AI hardware fading / high-to-low rotation the defining tape label.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 0.97 sits near neutral, but QQQ at 0.88 and IWM at 2.34 reveal complacent Nasdaq positioning versus heavy small-cap protection demand. Positive IV skew across SPY +11.8%, QQQ +11.1%, and IWM +10.5% shows persistent put premium and downside hedging appetite, aligning with VIX 21.5, VXN 30.5, and RVX 26.3 all above their 20-day MAs and signaling a jump in perceived tail risk.

CROSS-ASSET — The key transmission chain was the surge in Treasury yields dragging long-duration assets and precious metals, with IEF -0.53%, TLT -0.51%, GLD -3.65%, and SLV -8.08% all hit as higher real rates pressured both growth equity valuations and non-yielding hedges. Dollar strength via UUP +0.65% added an extra headwind to commodities and EM proxies while HYG -0.50% and LQD -0.62% showed credit bending, not breaking, under the macro shock.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 61.9 prints “Greed” even as SOXX -10.44%, XLK -6.66%, and SLV -8.08% were the marquee momentum unwinds, with XLP the best 20-day reversal sector and XLC the weakest 20-day laggard. WGMI -11.20% on 3.5x volume signals outright distribution in crypto-miner beta, while MSOS +7.25% on 2.7x volume looks like aggressive accumulation in beaten-down cannabis. EWH +1.53% with 2.9x volume and PPH +0.70% with 4.1x volume flag regionals and pharma as quiet relative winners under the surface. For tomorrow, QQQE -4.27% on 2.8x volume deserves attention as equal-weight Nasdaq de-grossing accelerates, DBA -1.09% on 3.2x volume is a key read on ag-commodity liquidation, and SLV -8.08% vs GEX_flip 55.0 highlights an options-driven air pocket in silver that could keep volatility elevated.
